sections.title: Leadership
sections.summary: CEO V. James Vena and CFO Jennifer L. Hamann lead a management team that delivered an 8% EPS increase and a 59.8% operating ratio in 2025, alongside best-ever safety results. Operations EVP Eric G. Gehringer oversaw an 8% improvement in freight car velocity and a 9-point Service Performance Index gain for intermodal.
points: Vena and Hamann delivered FY2025 net income of $6.379 billion on $24.119 billion in revenue, with a 10-basis-point operating ratio improvement to 59.8%.
Under Gehringer's operations leadership, terminal dwell declined 8% and the derailment incident rate improved 19% to 1.75, marking best-ever safety performance.

sections.title: Capital Allocation
sections.summary: Management executed an accelerated share repurchase program across Q1 and Q2 2025 while maintaining $8.379 billion in operating cash flow. The pending Norfolk Southern acquisition, expected to issue approximately 224.8 million shares and pay $20 billion in cash, signals a major capital deployment shift toward network expansion.
points: The company initiated an accelerated share repurchase program on February 18, 2025, with additional activity in Q1 and Q2 2025, returning capital while operating with $31.814 billion in long-term debt.
The Norfolk Southern merger agreement includes a $2.5 billion termination fee, and UNP incurred $72 million in acquisition-related expenses in 2025, reflecting significant deal execution risk.

sections.title: Shareholder Alignment
sections.summary: Equity compensation plans include 2,487,690 outstanding securities with a weighted-average exercise price of $210.32 and 29,874,539 securities available for future issuance. The advisory vote on executive compensation at the May 2026 annual meeting provides shareholders a say-on-pay mechanism.
points: Approximately 593.4 million shares were outstanding as of January 2026, with non-affiliate market value of $136.1 billion as of June 2025, indicating broad institutional ownership.
Operations EVP Gehringer adopted a Rule 10b5-1 plan to sell up to 1,999 shares between March and December 2026, a modest planned divestiture relative to total holdings.

sections.title: Communication
sections.summary: Union Pacific reports revenue across three principal segments-Bulk, Industrial, and Premium-plus Other subsidiary and accessorial revenues, with detailed operating metrics including freight car velocity, terminal dwell, and Service Performance Index scores. The 10-K and DEF 14A provide granular volume mix data and adjusted non-GAAP measures excluding acquisition costs and one-time items.
points: The company discloses adjusted FY2025 results excluding acquisition-related expense, industrial park land sales, deferred tax adjustments, and a crew staffing agreement, yielding adjusted diluted EPS of $11.66 and an adjusted operating ratio of 59.3%.

Segment reporting breaks out volume drivers including coal, industrial chemicals and plastics, grain, and rock shipments, partially offset by weaker automotive and energy and specialized markets demand.

sections.title: Governance
sections.summary: An eleven-member board including directors David B. Dillon, Doyle R. Simons, and Sheri H. Edison oversees the company, with Deloitte & Touche LLP providing an unqualified internal controls opinion. The 2026 annual meeting includes director elections, auditor ratification, and an advisory executive compensation vote.
points: Deloitte & Touche LLP issued an unqualified opinion on internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2025, with management assessing controls effective under the COSO 2013 framework.
The STB's January 16, 2026 decision that the Norfolk Southern merger application was incomplete represents a material regulatory risk, with the acquisition now expected to close in 2027.

types.summary: Union Pacific operates a line-haul railroad generating $24.119 billion in FY2025 revenue across Bulk, Industrial, and Premium segments, with an operating ratio of 59.8% that reflects the cost advantages of moving high volumes over a fixed rail network. The pending Norfolk Southern acquisition would extend this network by combining two Class I railroads.

The STB's review of the Norfolk Southern merger-requiring a revised application after the January 2026 incomplete determination-illustrates the high regulatory barriers that protect existing Class I railroad franchises from new entrants. These same barriers also pose acquisition execution risk, with completion now expected in 2027.

With $8.379 billion in operating cash flow and a $2.0 billion equity-method investment in TTX Company for rail car pooling, Union Pacific leverages scale to manage equipment costs of $447 million annually while serving diverse commodity volumes across coal, chemicals, grain, and intermodal freight.

Customers shipping coal, industrial chemicals and plastics, grain, and rock via Union Pacific's network face logistical and contractual costs to reroute to alternative transportation, supporting the 1% volume growth in 2025 despite weaker demand in automotive and energy and specialized markets.
